steps.title: Use the sign pattern
steps.content: The function $f(x)=(x-a)(x-b)$ opens upward. It is negative between its two roots and positive outside them.

steps.title: Locate the roots
steps.content: The values go positive at $15$, negative at $18$, and positive at $21$. Therefore, one root is between $15$ and $18$, and the other is between $18$ and $21$.

steps.title: Use integer roots
steps.content: Because $a$ and $b$ are integers, the possible roots are $16$ or $17$ for the first interval and $19$ or $20$ for the second interval. The possible sums are $$16+19=35,\quad 16+20=36,\quad 17+19=36,\quad 17+20=37$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Only $35$ appears among the choices, so D is correct.
